back_translation: While Apollos was at the city of Corinth, Paul traveled through the upper country and arrived at the city of Ephesus. There he found some disciples
and asked them, 'When you believed, did you receive the Holy Spirit?' They answered, 'We have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.'
He asked further, 'Then into what were you baptized?' They replied, 'Into the baptism of John.'
Paul then said, 'John administered the baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the One who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.'
When they heard this, they received baptism into the name of the Lord Jesus.
When Paul laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came down upon them, and they spoke in other tongues and prophesied.
They were in total about twelve men.
Paul entered the synagogue and proclaimed boldly for three months, reasoning and persuading concerning the kingdom of God.

But when some were becoming hardened of heart and refused to believe, speaking evil of 'the Way' in front of the crowd, he withdrew from them and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.
This continued for two years, so that all those dwelling in the province of Asia heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.
God performed extraordinary miracles through the hands of Paul,
so that handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body were carried to the sick, and diseases left them and evil spirits departed from them.
Now there were some Jewish men who were itinerant exorcists who tried to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those possessed by evil spirits, saying, 'I command you by Jesus whom Paul proclaims.'
Those who did this were the seven sons of a Jewish man named Sceva, who was a chief priest.
But the evil spirit answered them, 'Jesus I recognize, and Paul I am acquainted with, but who are you?'
Then the man in whom the evil spirit dwelt leapt upon them, overpowered them all, and prevailed against them, so that they fled from that house naked and wounded.
This event became known among all the Jews and Greeks who lived in the city of Ephesus, and fear came upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified and praised.
Many of those who had believed came confessing and disclosing their deeds.

A considerable number of those who had practiced magic arts brought their books and burned them in front of everyone. When the value of those books was calculated, it came to fifty thousand silver pieces.
By the power of the Lord, the word grew and spread and prevailed.

When these events had been accomplished, Paul resolved in the Spirit to travel through the province of Macedonia and the province of Achaia and go to Jerusalem, saying, 'After I have been there, I must see Rome as well.'
So he sent two of his helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to the province of Macedonia, while he himself stayed for some time in the province of Asia.
At that time a great disturbance arose concerning 'the Way.'
There was a silversmith named Demetrius who made silver shrines in the form of the goddess Artemis, bringing considerable income to the craftsmen.
He gathered them and the workers in related trades to meet together, and said, 'Men, you know that our income comes from this trade.'

'You can see and hear that not only in Ephesus, but in nearly the whole province of Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away the minds of many people, saying: Idols made by human hands are not gods.'

'Not only will our trade fall into disrepute, but even the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be reckoned as nothing, and the majesty of the goddess whom all the province of Asia and the whole world worship will be deposed.'
When they heard this they were filled with rage, and shouted, 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!'
The whole city was filled with confusion. They rushed together into the theater, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus the Macedonians, Paul's traveling companions.
Paul wanted to enter and speak before the crowd, but the disciples would not let him go.
Even some of the high officials of the province of Asia who were Paul's friends sent word to him, pleading with him not to enter the theater.
At that time some were shouting one thing, some another, for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not even know why they had gathered there.
The Jews pushed Alexander out from the crowd, and Alexander motioned with his hand for silence, intending to give a defense to the populace.
But when they realized that he was a Jew, one voice arose from all of them, and they shouted for about two hours, 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!'

When the city clerk quieted the crowd, he said, 'Men of Ephesus, who among humans does not know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of the image that fell from heaven?'
'Since these things are undeniably accepted, you ought to be calm of mind and not do anything rash.'
'For you have brought these men here, even though they have not robbed the temple and have not blasphemed our goddess.'
'Therefore if Demetrius and the craftsmen with him have a case against anyone, the courts are open and there are proconsuls available; let them bring charges against one another there.'
'But if you seek anything more, that matter must be considered in a lawful assembly.'
'For we are in danger of being charged with rioting over today's events, and we have no justification to explain this commotion.' When he had said these things, he released the assembly to end.
